scope: K16xD16 BF16 MFMA contribution tile for compressed sparse-MLA dKV
target_kernel_direction: same-workgroup or persistent producer-consumer sparse-attention backward owner: q,dO,kv,indices,lse/sink -> dQ + group-aware dKV
why_this_probe_exists: The prior HIP/rocWMMA score-page probe proved the same-workgroup score-tile idea but was too slow as a dKV-only emitter. This probe checked whether CK Tile's gfx950 BF16 MFMA attribute could be used as the low-level contribution fragment inside the next cooperative owner.

build_result.reason_for_split_source: Avoid PyTorch HIP extension Thrust header duplicate-definition collision by compiling the CK/HIP object with raw hipcc, then linking it into a tiny PyTorch binding.

evidence.read: First split-source CK build ran fast but failed parity with equal reference/candidate norms, suggesting lane-layout mismatch rather than build failure.
C-lane store adjusted from CK constants but still failed parity.
A/B lane-feed variant made norm drift worse; not the right fix.
Six direct C-store layouts all failed parity; median micro timings were around 0.0077-0.0093 ms, but correctness failed for every mode.

decision.do_not_promote: Do not wire the half-manual CK WarpGemmAttributeMfma fragment into the sparse-attention owner yet.
continue_with: Direct HIP/rocWMMA or direct MFMA intrinsics with an explicitly parity-proven lane layout.
Or a fuller CK Tile block/warp tensor API path that loads, computes, and stores through CK distributions end-to-end rather than mixing CK MFMA attributes with manual fragment packing.
keep_from_this_probe: The raw hipcc prebuild plus PyTorch binding pattern works and avoids the PyTorch HIP header collision.
The correct target is still a cooperative source-boundary sparse backward owner, not a late dKV-only replacement.
decision.next_gate: Build the query-major cooperative source-boundary sparse-MLA backward kernel with resident score/prob/scaled-dP tile and group-aware dKV epilogue; measure actual S4096 sparse-attention backward boundary latency against the 11.13 ms Triton boundary and then e2e TFLOPs.
